questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which statement about galaxies is correct?
options: A galaxy is a single star with many planets around it.
A galaxy is smaller than a solar system.
A galaxy can exist only inside Earth's atmosphere.
A galaxy is a large collection of stars held together by gravity.
questions.correctAnswer: A galaxy is a large collection of stars held together by gravity.
questions.explanation: Galaxies contain billions of stars, gas and dust, all held together by gravity.
questions.senNote: Galaxies contain billions of stars, gas and dust, all held together by gravity.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: On a clinic worksheet, a learner checks: a student points to a huge group of stars and dust held together by gravity. What is this called?
options: One star with one moon.
A small asteroid.
A galaxy is a large collection of stars held together by gravity.
A crater on the Moon.
questions.correctAnswer: A galaxy is a large collection of stars held together by gravity.
questions.explanation: A galaxy is a large collection of stars held together by gravity.
questions.senNote: A galaxy is a large collection of stars held together by gravity.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: On a museum label, a visitor reads: a results table says Andromeda contains many stars, gas and dust. Which object is being described?
options: A cloud in Earth air.
A galaxy is a large collection of stars held together by gravity.
A comet tail.
A layer of the atmosphere.
questions.correctAnswer: A galaxy is a large collection of stars held together by gravity.
questions.explanation: A galaxy is a large collection of stars held together by gravity.
questions.senNote: A galaxy is a large collection of stars held together by gravity.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: On a school lab card, a pupil answers: which answer names a very large collection of stars held together by gravity?
options: A galaxy is a large collection of stars held together by gravity.
A small asteroid.
A group of satellites.
A single planet.
questions.correctAnswer: A galaxy is a large collection of stars held together by gravity.
questions.explanation: A galaxy is a large collection of stars held together by gravity.
questions.senNote: A galaxy is a large collection of stars held together by gravity.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Recall the life cycle of a Sun-like star
questions.question: What happens to a star like the Sun after the red giant stage?
options: It stops all nuclear change instantly.
It turns into a planet.
It changes into a galaxy.
It forms a planetary nebula and then becomes a white dwarf.
questions.correctAnswer: It forms a planetary nebula and then becomes a white dwarf.
questions.explanation: A Sun-like star becomes a red giant, then a planetary nebula, and finally a white dwarf.
questions.senNote: A Sun-like star becomes a red giant, then a planetary nebula, and finally a white dwarf.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Recall the life cycle of a Sun-like star
questions.question: On a clinic worksheet, a learner checks: a student orders the life cycle of a star like the Sun. What comes after red giant?
options: It stops all nuclear change instantly.
It turns into a planet.
It forms a planetary nebula and then becomes a white dwarf.
It becomes a black hole at once.
questions.correctAnswer: It forms a planetary nebula and then becomes a white dwarf.
questions.explanation: A Sun-like star forms a planetary nebula and becomes a white dwarf.
questions.senNote: A Sun-like star forms a planetary nebula and becomes a white dwarf.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Recall the life cycle of a Sun-like star
questions.question: On a museum label, a visitor reads: a results table shows red giant, then planetary nebula. What final object forms?
options: It stops all nuclear change instantly.
It forms a planetary nebula and then becomes a white dwarf.
It turns into a planet.
It stays red forever.
questions.correctAnswer: It forms a planetary nebula and then becomes a white dwarf.
questions.explanation: A Sun-like star forms a planetary nebula and becomes a white dwarf.
questions.senNote: A Sun-like star forms a planetary nebula and becomes a white dwarf.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Recall the life cycle of a Sun-like star
questions.question: On a school lab card, a pupil answers: what happens near the end of the life of a star with about the Sun's mass?
options: It forms a planetary nebula and then becomes a white dwarf.
It stops all nuclear change instantly.
It turns into a planet.
It explodes as every type of supernova.
questions.correctAnswer: It forms a planetary nebula and then becomes a white dwarf.
questions.explanation: A Sun-like star forms a planetary nebula and becomes a white dwarf.
questions.senNote: A Sun-like star forms a planetary nebula and becomes a white dwarf.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Why are optical telescopes often placed on mountains or in space?
options: Space removes the need for detectors.
Stars become closer.
Mirrors stop reflecting.
There is less atmosphere to distort or absorb the light.
questions.correctAnswer: There is less atmosphere to distort or absorb the light.
questions.explanation: Less atmosphere means clearer observations because there is less absorption and distortion of light.
questions.senNote: Less atmosphere means clearer observations because there is less absorption and distortion of light.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: On a clinic worksheet, a learner checks: a student compares ground and space telescopes. Why is space clearer?
options: Space removes the need for detectors.
Stars become closer.
There is less atmosphere to distort or absorb the light.
The Sun powers every image.
questions.correctAnswer: There is less atmosphere to distort or absorb the light.
questions.explanation: A telescope above the atmosphere avoids much absorption and distortion.
questions.senNote: A telescope above the atmosphere avoids much absorption and distortion.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: On a museum label, a visitor reads: a results table says some wavelengths are absorbed by air. Why place a telescope in orbit?
options: Space removes the need for detectors.
There is less atmosphere to distort or absorb the light.
Stars become closer.
Lenses become weightless.
questions.correctAnswer: There is less atmosphere to distort or absorb the light.
questions.explanation: A telescope above the atmosphere avoids much absorption and distortion.
questions.senNote: A telescope above the atmosphere avoids much absorption and distortion.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: On a school lab card, a pupil answers: why do space telescopes avoid much of the blurring caused by the atmosphere?
options: There is less atmosphere to distort or absorb the light.
Space removes the need for detectors.
Stars become closer.
The telescope makes its own stars.
questions.correctAnswer: There is less atmosphere to distort or absorb the light.
questions.explanation: A telescope above the atmosphere avoids much absorption and distortion.
questions.senNote: A telescope above the atmosphere avoids much absorption and distortion.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: On a factory safety note, an apprentice reviews: a satellite telescope observes faint galaxies. What advantage does its position give?
options: Space removes the need for detectors.
Stars become closer.
Gravity bends no light at all.
There is less atmosphere to distort or absorb the light.
questions.correctAnswer: There is less atmosphere to distort or absorb the light.
questions.explanation: A telescope above the atmosphere avoids much absorption and distortion.
questions.senNote: A telescope above the atmosphere avoids much absorption and distortion.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: On a space centre display, a guide explains: which reason explains why some telescopes are launched into space?
options: Space removes the need for detectors.
Stars become closer.
There is less atmosphere to distort or absorb the light.
The Moon blocks all clouds.
questions.correctAnswer: There is less atmosphere to distort or absorb the light.
questions.explanation: A telescope above the atmosphere avoids much absorption and distortion.
questions.senNote: A telescope above the atmosphere avoids much absorption and distortion.

questions.explanation: A telescope above the atmosphere avoids much absorption and distortion.

questions.explanation: A telescope above the atmosphere avoids much absorption and distortion.
questions.senNote: A telescope above the atmosphere avoids much absorption and distortion.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Why do astronauts appear weightless in orbit?
options: Air resistance above Earth pushes the satellite around the orbit.
The satellite engine must keep firing all the time to hold the orbit.
There is no gravity in space, so the satellite floats in a straight line.
They are in free-fall while moving forwards around Earth.
questions.correctAnswer: They are in free-fall while moving forwards around Earth.
questions.explanation: Astronauts and the spacecraft are both falling together around Earth, so they appear weightless.
questions.senNote: Astronauts and the spacecraft are both falling together around Earth, so they appear weightless.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: What does red-shift suggest about many distant galaxies?
options: They are becoming cooler than all nearby stars only.
They are moving towards us faster than light.
They have stopped emitting electromagnetic waves.
They are moving away from us.
questions.correctAnswer: They are moving away from us.
questions.explanation: Red-shift shows that light has stretched, which happens when a source is moving away from us.
questions.senNote: Red-shift shows that light has stretched, which happens when a source is moving away from us.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: On a clinic worksheet, a learner checks: a student sees galaxy absorption lines at longer wavelengths. What motion does this show?
options: They have stopped shining.
They are all blue stars.
They are moving away from us.
They are smaller than atoms.
questions.correctAnswer: They are moving away from us.
questions.explanation: Red-shift shows light has been stretched, so the galaxy is moving away.
questions.senNote: Red-shift shows light has been stretched, so the galaxy is moving away.

questions.explanation: Red-shift shows light has been stretched, so the galaxy is moving away.
questions.senNote: Red-shift shows light has been stretched, so the galaxy is moving away.
